name: Ria Money Transfer
description_short: At 4723 Bergenline Ave, Union City, NJ 07087, Ria Money Transfer can be reached at +1 855-355-2141.
description_long:
Ria Money Transfer operates as a global remittance and money transfer service under the regulatory oversight of Dandelion Payments, Inc. (NMLS ID #920968). The company facilitates cross-border financial transactions for individuals and businesses seeking to move money internationally with multiple delivery options. The platform supports transfers to major regions including Africa, Asia, Europe, Latin America, North America, and Oceania, with operations spanning countries from Argentina to the United Kingdom.

Ria's service offerings include multiple sending methods: online transfers, mobile app transfers, in-person agent locations, and WhatsApp-based transfers. On the receiving end, customers can access funds through bank deposits, cash pickups at agent locations, digital wallet credits, home delivery, and ATM withdrawals. The company provides supplementary financial tools including a currency converter and IBAN calculator to assist customers in planning transfers. Their mobile application enables on-the-go transaction management and transfer tracking capabilities.

Ria distinguishes itself through its omnichannel approach, offering both digital and physical transaction options. The WhatsApp transfer capability represents a modern integration for users preferring messaging-based financial services. The company operates an agent network for in-person services and provides multiple payout options, accommodating different recipient preferences and banking infrastructure availability across receiving countries.

Limitations include the inaccessibility of detailed company information on their website—pages for "About Ria" and specific service details returned 404 errors during research. Specific fee structures, exchange rates, delivery timelines, and regulatory disclosures are not transparently presented in available web content. The company's positioning within the check-cashing category reflects its money transfer function, though the service extends significantly beyond traditional check-cashing into international remittance operations.
pros: Operates in 70+ countries across six continents (Africa, Asia, Europe, Latin America, North America, Oceania)
Multiple sending channels including online, mobile app, in-person locations, and WhatsApp transfers
Diverse receiving options: bank deposits, cash pickup, digital wallet, home delivery, and ATM withdrawal
Mobile app available for tracking transfers and managing transactions on-the-go
Free financial tools including currency converter and IBAN calculator for transfer planning
Regulated money transmitter with NMLS ID #920968 under Dandelion Payments, Inc.
Agent partnership program allowing third-party location access for in-person services
cons: Website lacks transparency on fee structures, exchange rates, and delivery timeframes
Critical company information pages (About, Services) return 404 errors, limiting consumer research
No publicly accessible details on transfer limits, processing times, or eligibility requirements
Limited disclosure of regulatory compliance information and consumer protections on primary website
WhatsApp transfer capability may not be available in all countries or for all transaction types
best_for: International workers and immigrants sending remittances to family in multiple countries
Individuals preferring digital-first money transfer options with mobile app functionality
Customers seeking cash pickup options in countries with limited banking infrastructure
Users comfortable with WhatsApp-based transfers for frequent, smaller international payments
diagnosis: Ria Money Transfer is best for international remittance senders and recipients seeking flexible, multi-channel options across 70+ countries, particularly those in regions with limited banking access who prefer cash pickup or home delivery. Primary caveat: critical pricing, fee, and service detail information is not transparently available on the website, requiring customers to contact agents directly for specific transaction terms.
services: In-person money transfer at agent locations
